description: Genome assembly accession or isolate accession used as the primary sample identifier.
NCBI BioSample accession when available.
Organism name reported in the source metadata.
Original isolation-source text from NCBI or harmonized metadata.
Host information when available.
Collection date or year when available.
Geographic location information when available.
Harmonized training source category.
example_or_allowed_values: Beef, Chicken, Pork, Seafood, Dairy, Fruit, Vegetable, Soil, Water

description: Dataset membership indicator.
Seed-genome dataset indicator when present.
Dataset context for the mapping rule.
Target source category or group name.
Rule priority used during source-category assignment.
Case-insensitive regular-expression terms used to screen source text.
Additional notes for the mapping rule.
Sample accession or isolate identifier.
Source label when included in the matrix.
Allele call for a cgMLST or wgMLST locus. Missing or failed calls may be encoded as -1 depending on preprocessing.
Rank of the wgMLST locus after source-signal scoring.
wgMLST locus identifier.
Source-label association score or normalized mutual-information component, if present.
Within-source versus between-source separation component, if present.
Proportion of isolates with valid allele calls for the locus, if present.
Final combined ranking score used to order loci, if present.
Rank of the locus within the Top800 feature set, if present.
Identifier of a Top800 wgMLST locus.
wgMLST locus rank when available.
wgMLST locus identifier.
Best Swiss-Prot or DIAMOND hit identifier when available.
Annotated gene name when available.
Annotated protein or product name when available.
Assigned broad functional category.
R scripts are executable analysis files rather than tabular data.
